facts.text: Heathcote’s name comes from Old English hēath, meaning “heather” or “wasteland,” and cote, meaning a cottage or shelter, hinting at a humble dwelling on open land.
The area that is now Heathcote began as farmland in Middlesex County before evolving into a suburban corridor thanks to its proximity to Princeton and New Brunswick.
Heathcote functions mainly as a residential enclave, drawing cultural and educational influence from nearby Rutgers University and the historic scholarly environment of Princeton.
The community sits on a slope that drains south toward Heathcote Brook, a west‑flowing tributary of the Millstone River in the Raritan watershed.
Route 1 cuts through the eastern side of Heathcote, linking the town northeast to New Brunswick and southwest to Trenton.
County Route 522, known locally as Promenade Boulevard, runs east–west through Heathcote, connecting Routes 1 and 27 across the township.
